flares.note: Simultaneous flaring is also observed from Active Region 14480 (S14W38) peaking around 2026-07-01T19:35Z.
Simultaneous flaring from Active Region 14479 (N19W47) and Active Region 14478 (S06W34)
Previously attributed to AR 14479 (N15W77), which appears to have a simultaneous flare starting slightly earlier as seen in SDO AIA 131 imagery.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NE in STEREO A COR2 and the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The CME is likely obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and is not visible. The potential source is an eruption seen in SDO AIA 193 around N15E20 as dimming starting at 2026-06-29T02:42Z. Opening field lines are also visible near the NE limb in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location. Limited imagery was available for a measurement due to the faint leading edge.

cmes.note: Faint CME seen to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and the SSW of STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is a filament eruption centered around S35W100 as seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery starting at 2026-06-29T04:45Z. The eruption is also visible off the southwest limb in GOES SUVI 304 and SDO AIA 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on potential source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT. The leading edge is not clearly measurable in GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and faintly to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME is not seen in real-time in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink data gap. The source is likely farsided as seen as moving field lines in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA and SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-06-29T06:57Z. An estimated M4.0 class solar flare was observed on the farside from the point of view of the Solar Orbiter/STIX, located around longitude -158, at 2026-06-29T07:29Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The leading edge is faint and unclear in running difference imagery, and this measurement is approximate.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and NW in STEREO A COR2, likely obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely in the vicinity of AR 14479 (N16W03), observed as field line brightening and movement followed by dimming in SDO AIA 193 starting as early as 2026-06-29T09:26Z spanning to approximately 2026-06-29T14:00Z, centered ~N15E05. Also observed here in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting at 2026-06-29T11:05Z. This source timing is early, but slow to evolve, so still a good candidate that aligns with triangulation. Partially overlapped by following CME 2026-06-29T17:36Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and NW in STEREO A COR2, likely obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely in the vicinity of AR 14479 (N16W07), observed as field line brightening and movement followed by dimming in SDO AIA 171/193 starting as early around 2026-06-29T17:18Z, centered ~N13W05. Also observed here in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting at 2026-06-29T17:25Z. Partially overlapped by prior CME 2026-06-29T16:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2, and faintly to the W in SOHO LASCO C2. The source is likely an eruption in the vicinity of former AR 14471, located approximately N17W140 at this time. Likely observed in STEREO A EUVI 195 at 2026-06-29T17:15Z as a brief dimming and field line movement near the NW limb from STEREO A.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between STEREO A COR2, and the very faint front in SOHO LASCO C2 better observed in running difference imagery.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NNE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, likely obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 and not in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap starting at 2026-06-29T19:38Z. The source is associated with an M1.4 class flare from AR 14479 peaking at 2026-06-29T21:40Z observed in SDO AIA 131. Also observed in SDO AIA 171/193 as brightening, field line movement, and slight dimming following the flare. The CME front appears deflected slightly NE from the source region.

cmeAnalyses.note: Single spacecraft 3D analysis as SOHO LASCO C2. Using the source location at N16W04, we find the front deflected NE, and without the ability to triangulate in real-time. As such, this region's eruptive history is referenced, and a latitude of N30 then fitting the longitude to E03 is used to approximate the parameters, and may vary as a result. This deflection does fit with the observed wide opening field lines in SDO AIA 193.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely an eruption from Active Region 14479 (N16W15) starting around 2026-06-30T13:30Z which exhibits northern deflection as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304 imagery. Dimming is observed northeast of the eruption site in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Full halo CME currently observed only in GOES CCOR-1 and somewhat blocked by its pylon. Not yet observed in other coronagraphs due to a SOHO LASCO DSN gap between 2026-06-30T20:15Z to 2026-06-30T23:45Z, and STEREO A data gap starting at 2026-06-30T19:23Z. The source is associated with an X1.1 flare (N17W18) observed in SDO AIA 131 from AR 14479 (N16W22), peaking at 2026-06-30T20:50Z. Also observed as a NE-deflected EUV wave and wide swath of dimming in SDO AIA 193/211 centered around N20W05, brightening/flare ribbons along a channel to the NE of the region in SDO AIA 131/171/193/211/304, and a post-eruptive brightening starting around 2026-06-30T21:46Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary shock analysis with only several available GOES CCOR-1 frames. Due to the limited frames and full halo presentation, the shock width could vary, impacting speed. Current preliminary analysis of the shock is consistently around 1200 km/s, with high confidence in the latitude and longitude.

Updated 3D single spacecraft analysis using SOHO LASCO C3. STEREO A COR2 returns from a data gap at 2026-07-01T00:09Z and observes the CME material but the LE is just beyond the FOV at that time and cannot be tracked. This is enough to use COR2 to estimate the more northward latitude than preliminary analysis and estimate the half width, while no frames are officially included in the analysis.

Preliminary bulk anaylsis with only several available GOES CCOR-1 frames. Due to the limited frames and full halo presentation, the CME width could vary, impacting speed. Current preliminary analysis of the bulk is consistently around 900-1000 km/s, with high confidence in the latitude and longitude.

cmes.note: Feature first seen to the ENE in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-07-01T00:38Z. It is also possibly visible in SOHO LASCO C2 but not traceably so. There is no separate source candidate for this front; it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-06-30T21:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement with STEREO A COR2 due to lack of triangulation and lack of source candidate.

cmes.note: Feature first seen to the ENE in STEREO A COR2 starting at 2026-07-01T03:23Z. It is also possibly visible in SOHO LASCO C2 but not traceably so. There is no separate source candidate for this front; it is most likely outflow following CME: 2026-06-30T21:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement with STEREO A COR2 due to lack of triangulation and lack of source candidate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-07-01T03:48Z. It is also seen to the E in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is most likely opening field lines on or slightly beyond the E limb best seen in SDO AIA 193 starting around 2026-07-01T03:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of triangulation and source on/beyond E limb.

cmes.note: Very faint front to the NNW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-07-01T12:12Z for a couple of frames before the start of a gap between downlink periods. The start time is somewhat ambiguous due to the faintness and overlap with an earlier feature to the N. This CME is also seen to the NNW in GOES CCOR-1. It is covered by real-time data gaps in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The source of this CME is most likely associated with an M1.0 class flare from AR 14479 (N13W33) that peaked at 2026-07-01T11:25Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131. The accompanying eruption including dimming, opening field lines, and an EUV wave is best seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Speculative, PRELIMINARY measurement based on early frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 as well as on visible source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-07-01T16:23Z. This CME is not seen in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink data gap from 2026-07-01T12:48Z to 2026-07-01T18:47Z, and is not clearly seen in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is a long duration M2.6 flare from Active Region 14479 (N16W32) which peaked at 2026-07-01T14:54Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Dimming, field line opening and post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193, 211 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the East by STEREO A COR2 beginning at 2026-07-01T17:23Z. This CME is not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 due to a downlink data gap from 2026-07-01T12:48Z to 2026-07-01T18:47Z, and is not clearly seen in GOES CCOR-1. There is not clear source seen for this event, and lack of SOHO and GOES imagery makes  triangulation difficult.

cmeAnalyses.note: STEREO A plane-of-sky using a longitude of E27 due to lack of clear source and no GOES or SOHO imagery to triangulate source.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the North by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-02T00:06Z, following a data gap which ended at 2026-07-01T23:54Z. This CME is also seen to the North in SOHO LASCO C3 and to the Northeast in STEREO A COR2 following a data gap which ended at 2026-07-02T00:08Z. This CME is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is an M3.5 flare from Active Region 14479 (N15W32) which peaked at 2026-07-01T19:43Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Field line opening and post eruptive arcades are also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-01T21:24Z. This fronts fades before exiting the field of view in C2, and is therefore not visible in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 or GOES CCOR-1. The source of this event is an M2.0 from Active Region 14480 (S14W38) which peaked at 2026-07-01T19:35Z as seen in SDO AIA 131, along with post eruptive arcades seen in SDO AIA 171 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME associated with M4.2-class solar flare in Active Region 14799 located near N17W39; the CME is comprised of a separate brighter bulk seen to the north in SOHO LASCO C2 and seen to the east-northeast in STEREO A COR2; the fainter shock is extremely wide relative to the bulk and is seen faintly all the way from due west to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2 and from the north to the southeast in STEREO A COR2A. The eruption is characterized by a circular shock front seen in EUV imagery, particularly in GOES SUVI 284 imagery as there was an ongoing SDO AIA outage at the time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge/bulk measurement using only SOHO/STEREO A as the CME is obscured by the pylon in GOES. Strong eastward deflection along the Earth-Sun line with high confidence as this is visually confirmed in EUV imagery.
Shock front incorporating very wide shock feature seen to the west in LASCO and to the southeast in STEREO A COR2A simultaneously. To do so requires a width >50 degrees.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2 and E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. No source is observed directly for this CME, likely on the eastern far-side from Earth. Partially overlaps with CME: 2026-07-02T09:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and SE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. No source is observed directly for this CME, likely on the eastern far-side from Earth. Partially overlaps with CME: 2026-07-02T08:53Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Minor, narrow CME seen to the north-northwest in SOHO LASCO C2 and to the northeast in STEREO A COR2A which could be associated with a minor eruption on the Earth-facing disk seen near the vicinity of Active Region 14479 (N17W47) or farther to the east seen near 2026-07-02T11:20Z best in GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement of the CME using SOHO C2, a few frames of SOHO C3, and STEREO A COR2A.

cmes.note: Faint CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2, briefly in C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be moving/opening field lines visible along the NE limb in SDO/AIA 193 and 171 starting around 2026-07-02T22:00Z. This feature overlaps the CME: 2026-07-03T02:00Z in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There may be some uncertainty with parameters due to overlapping features from the perspective of STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: This slow CME is visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 imagery and NE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. It is obscured by the control arm in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Very narrow CME visible to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. It is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. The CME is slightly more narrow than the measurement constraint of 10 degrees half-width.

cmes.note: Narrow CME visible to the W in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky parameters based on best fit in STEREO A COR2 imagery using a longitude of 153 degrees.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-07-03T14:24Z. This CME fades before reaching the edge of the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 and is not seen in STEREO A COR2 due to a real-time gap between downlink periods. The most likely source candidate is a wide loop and opening field lines seen beyond the W limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning around 2026-07-03T14:17Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference imagery from SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-07-03T18:36Z. It is also seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 and to the NNE in STEREO A COR2. Only the first couple of frames in SOHO LASCO C2 are seen in real time due to a data gap between downlink periods. This CME is associated with an M6.7 class flare from AR 14479 (N15W58) that peaked at 2026-07-03T18:11Z. The flare is best seen in SDO AIA 131. A large EUV wave and wide opening field lines are also seen in SDO AIA 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: PRELIMINARY measurement based on best fit among GOES CCOR-1, STEREO A COR2, and early frames of SOHO LASCO C2 as well as on visible source location.
PRELIMINARY SHOCK measurement based only on source location and fit in GOES CCOR-1. Real-time data gaps in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 cover the majority of the propagation of the CME.

cmes.note: Narrow CME seen to the east in SOHO LASCO C2, faintly in GOES CCOR-1, and not well at all in STEREO A due to a data gap. The CME is likely associated with the M6.3-class solar flare from Active Region 14478 occurring near S10W53 with a narrow jet of plasma being seen erupting near the active region best in SDO AIA 131 and a large loop structure leaving the southwest limb in GOES SUVI 284 near 2026-07-03T19:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Leading edge measurement of the CME along the Sun-Earth line using only SOHO and GOES imagery and knowledge of the suspected source location, as the CME is not seen well in STEREO A imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The CME is partially obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 imagery and is not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME is likely farsided, with moving field lines visible beyond the NW limb in SDO AIA 171 in ISWA, SDO AIA 193, and GOES SUVI 284 imagery. Between a data gap from 2026-07-04T13:45Z to 2026-07-04T17:15Z, a filament on the west limb as seen from STEREO A EUV imagery (approximately N20W155) can be seen lifting off. The exact longitude of the filament is unclear as both footpoints are not clearly visible in EUV imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate potential source location using SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The CME is visible to the NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery after a data gap from 2026-07-04T19:53Z to 2026-07-05T00:09Z. The source location is likely farsided and not clearly visible in EUV imagery. One potential source may be a filament eruption seen erupting beyond the limb to the north in GOES SUVI 304 imagery around 2026-07-04T16:01Z. Another potential source candidate is opening field lines seen beyond the NE limb in GOES SUVI 284 and SDO AIA 171 starting at 2026-07-04T16:25Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT. Only one frame of STEREO A COR2 imagery was available for a measurement.

Plane of sky measurement using GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT. The corresponding leading edge is not clearly discernible in STEREO A COR2 imagery for a farsided source candidate in order to triangulate this CME in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME seen faintly to the NE in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME is not clearly visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The source is not clearly seen in available EUV imagery and is likely farsided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SW in GOES CCOR-1 imagery. There is a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time. There is a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery from 2026-07-04T19:53Z to 2026-07-05T00:09Z, but the CME is not clearly visible in available imagery. The likely source is an M1.5 flare from Active Region 14478 (S06W35) starting at 2026-07-04T19:23Z as seen in SDO AIA 131 imagery. Moving field lines and an ejecta of material is best seen in SDO AIA 193. The flare is also visible in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in GOES CCOR-1 and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. The CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery due to a data gap. The source is an X1.3 flare peaking at 2026-07-04T20:41Z from Active Region 14482 (S10E85) with an associated EUV wave and opening field lines as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated parameters based on best fit with respect to the source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Preliminary measurement based on source location using GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the NE in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and to the NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source for this CME is seen as opening field lines around 2026-07-04T23:05Z in GOES SUVI 284 imagery beyond the limb. There is outflowing material before and after this front.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Possible streamer blowout CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and to the N/NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME, however there are continued moving/opening field lines visible along the northern limb in GOES SUVI 284 imagery starting around 2026-07-05T00:00Z. There are numerous fronts and outflows ahead and behind of this CME, however the features behind this CME are included in this fit.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the W/NW in SOHO LASCO C2. It is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2. The source is a C9.9 flare from AR 14479 with associated filament ejecta visible to the W in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-07-05T07:13Z. The source is also visible in SDO/AIA and STEREO A EUVI wavelengths.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on source location and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 frames.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T11:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-07-05T10:09Z to 2026-07-05T17:23Z. There is no clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to likely far sided source and lack of multi-viewpoint imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T16:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-07-05T10:09Z to 2026-07-05T17:23Z. There is no clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to likely source beyond the East limb and lack of COR2A imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW beginning at 2026-07-05T17:00Z in SOHO LASCO C2, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this event is an M1.4 flare from AR 14479 (N16W88) peaking at 2026-07-05T16:32Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. A wide EUV wave and field line opening extending to the East of the flare (out to approx. East 50) is also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the East in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T17:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a small eruption seen located at approx. S09E18 beginning at approx. 2026-07-05T16:15Z as seen in SDO AIA 131 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T22:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is an M1.0 flare from AR 14479 (N16W91) which peaked at 2026-07-05T21:22Z as seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.
